crystal_ooze.name: crystal ooze
crystal_ooze.lore: A translucent, slow-moving ooze whose body refracts light into prismatic shards. Its crystalline pseudopods can slow victims with a coating of hardening mineral film. Miners fear these creatures for their habit of lurking in gem veins.

green_slime.name: green slime
green_slime.lore: Green slime clings to dungeon ceilings and drips onto unsuspecting prey. Contact with its acidic body quickly dissolves organic matter. Alchemists prize small samples for use in corrosive reagents.

yellow_mold.name: yellow mold
yellow_mold.lore: This bright fungal growth ejects clouds of hallucinogenic spores when disturbed. Adventurers struck by the cloud often wander aimlessly, laughing at invisible dangers. The spores are highly flammable and burn with a vivid orange flame.

brown_mold.name: brown mold
brown_mold.lore: Brown mold paradoxically absorbs heat from the surrounding air, chilling any creature that draws near. Fire used against it only makes it spread faster and grow colder. The crust it leaves on dungeon walls can be scraped off for use in cooling potions.

gibbering_slime.name: gibbering slime
gibbering_slime.lore: A pulsating mass that constantly emits maddening nonsense syllables from dozens of half-formed mouths. Prolonged exposure to its gibbering induces confusion and terror. Scholars debate whether it possesses some fractured form of intelligence.

acid_blob.name: acid blob
acid_blob.lore: An aggressive, fast-moving ooze that leaves trails of corroded stone and melted metal in its wake. Its acid can dissolve metal armor at an alarming rate. The concentrated bile inside its core is an alchemist's treasure.

toxic_jelly.name: toxic jelly
toxic_jelly.lore: A vibrant green jelly that carries a virulent toxin in every cell of its body. Any wound it inflicts quickly turns septic without magical treatment. Herbalists use the diluted jelly as a powerful antiseptic in small doses.

giant_scorpion.name: giant scorpion
giant_scorpion.lore: This horse-sized arachnid is among the deadliest creatures in the dungeon's middle depths. Its pincers can crush bones outright, while its stinger delivers a paralytic venom. Desert tribes craft armor from its chitinous shell.

giant_wasp.name: giant wasp
giant_wasp.lore: Giant wasps hunt in buzzing clouds and are fiercely territorial around their paper nests. A single sting delivers enough venom to fell a grown man in minutes. Their larval chambers sometimes contain the preserved bodies of paralyzed prey.

giant_beetle.name: giant beetle
giant_beetle.lore: Armored in a thick iridescent carapace, the giant beetle is a surprisingly durable foe. Its mandibles exert crushing force capable of biting through chain mail. Armorers prize the polished shell plates as decorative reinforcements.

giant_mantis.name: giant mantis
giant_mantis.lore: The giant mantis is a patient ambush predator that can remain perfectly motionless for hours. When it strikes, it moves with terrifying speed, its serrated forelegs capable of decapitating a man. Its compound eyes give it a nearly 360-degree field of vision.

spider_queen.name: spider queen
spider_queen.lore: The matriarch of spider colonies, the spider queen is an immense eight-legged nightmare covered in dense black hair. She communicates with lesser spiders through pheromones and can direct dozens of them simultaneously. Her throne room is an elaborate web-cathedral hung with cocooned prey.

phase_spider_lesser.name: phase spider lesser
phase_spider_lesser.lore: A smaller cousin of the true phase spider, capable of briefly stepping between planes to avoid attacks. It blinks in and out of visibility during combat, making it extremely difficult to strike. Its silk retains a faint magical shimmer even after the spider's death.

web_spinner.name: web spinner
web_spinner.lore: Web spinners are small but prolific spiders that festoon dungeon corridors with sticky traps. They prefer to let their webs do the fighting, retreating when prey proves too dangerous. Their silk is prized by tailors for its remarkable tensile strength.

death_beetle.name: death beetle
death_beetle.lore: The death beetle is a massive, obsidian-shelled horror associated with necromantic energies. Its carapace seems to absorb light itself, and its presence is heralded by an eerie rattling sound. Ancient cultures carved its likeness onto tomb doors to ward off grave robbers.

giant_lizard.name: giant lizard
giant_lizard.lore: Giant lizards are opportunistic predators that inhabit warm dungeon levels and cave systems. They rely on ambush tactics, remaining motionless until prey is within striking distance. Their meat is considered a delicacy in some underground settlements.

basilisk_lesser.name: lesser basilisk
basilisk_lesser.lore: A juvenile basilisk whose petrifying gaze is not yet fully developed, causing paralysis rather than true petrification. It is nonetheless an extremely dangerous predator whose rocky lair is usually littered with frozen prey. Herbalists seek its eyes for polymorph-related brews.

rock_python.name: rock python
rock_python.lore: Rock pythons are tremendous constrictors that lurk among boulders and tunnel crevices. They can swallow prey the size of a dwarf whole, though they prefer to crush victims first. Their thick, patterned scales are used to make exceptionally supple leather goods.

komodo_brute.name: komodo brute
komodo_brute.lore: A brutish giant lizard whose mouth teems with vile bacteria, making every bite a slow death sentence. It tracks wounded prey for miles, patiently waiting for infection to do its work. The jawbone of a komodo brute is sometimes used as a morbid trophy.

giant_frog.name: giant frog
giant_frog.lore: Giant frogs lurk in subterranean pools and wet tunnels, leaping upon prey with shocking speed. Their sticky tongues can snatch a torch from a hand at ten feet. In desperate times, their legs provide a passable meal.

mud_crawler.name: mud crawler
mud_crawler.lore: Mud crawlers are squat, heavily armored reptiles that hug subterranean waterways. Their thick mud-caked hide is nearly impervious to slashing weapons. They move slowly but are almost impossible to dislodge once they have secured a grip.

swamp_serpent.name: swamp serpent
swamp_serpent.lore: The swamp serpent is an aggressive aquatic snake that lurks in dungeon flooded chambers and underground rivers. Its potent hemotoxic venom causes rapid hemorrhaging in its victims. Local cultures use its shed skin as waterproofing material for boats and bags.

sea_snake.name: sea snake
sea_snake.lore: Sea snakes possess among the most potent neurotoxic venoms of any serpent, causing rapid paralysis. They are aggressive when cornered and capable of swimming faster than most men can run. Divers near coastal ruins have learned to fear these brightly banded hunters.

dire_lion.name: dire lion
dire_lion.lore: The dire lion is a magnificent and terrifying predator nearly twice the size of its mundane cousin. Its roar is said to shake loose stalactites from cave ceilings. Prides led by a dire lion claim enormous territories and drive off all competition.

dire_bear.name: dire bear
dire_bear.lore: Dire bears are primordial ursines the size of draft horses, possessed of immeasurable strength. They are normally reclusive but defend their territories with catastrophic violence. Their thick pelts are worth a fortune to the right furrier.

wolf_pack_leader.name: wolf pack leader
wolf_pack_leader.lore: Pack leaders are massive wolves that coordinate their brethren with complex howls and body language. They direct flanking maneuvers and coordinated takedowns with uncanny intelligence. Killing a pack leader often causes the rest of the pack to scatter.

werewolf.name: werewolf
werewolf.lore: A cursed human transformed by the moon's influence into a savage wolf-man hybrid. In hybrid form the werewolf possesses the cunning of a person and the ferocity of a beast. Its blood carries the lycanthropic curse, transmissible through bites.

werewolf_alpha.name: werewolf alpha
werewolf_alpha.lore: A centuries-old lycanthrope who has mastered the curse, maintaining control even under the full moon. The alpha can transform at will and commands every wolf and werewolf in its territory. Its howl has been known to drive even veteran warriors mad with fear.

werebear.name: werebear
werebear.lore: Werebears are among the more tragic lycanthropes, often struggling against their feral nature. In hybrid form they become immovable juggernauts capable of ripping iron doors from hinges. Unlike werewolves they do not seek victims, but their rage makes them no less deadly.

wererat.name: wererat
wererat.lore: Wererats skulk through sewer systems and dungeon middens, often working as thieves and informants. They prefer to avoid direct combat, using hit-and-run tactics and their knowledge of hidden passages. Their bite carries a diseased form of the lycanthropic curse.

weretiger.name: weretiger
weretiger.lore: The weretiger is the apex predator among lycanthropes, combining human cunning with the hunting mastery of a tiger. It is known to stalk prey for days, learning its patterns before striking with terrifying precision. Ancient eastern traditions revere the weretiger as a divine judge of the unworthy.

winter_wolf.name: winter wolf
winter_wolf.lore: Winter wolves are intelligent arctic beasts who can speak in a guttural language and breathe blasts of killing frost. They sometimes serve frost giants as hunters and trackers. Their beautiful white pelts are worth a king's ransom to wealthy furriers.

blink_dog.name: blink dog
blink_dog.lore: Blink dogs are intelligent teleporting canines that live in packs and are considered a good omen by travelers. They teleport short distances to attack and immediately blink away, making them extremely hard to pin down. Despite their chaotic fighting style they are deeply loyal creatures who can be befriended.

death_dog.name: death dog
death_dog.lore: Death dogs are two-headed fiendish hounds whose bite delivers a supernatural rotting disease. They are often found serving undead lords or guarding cursed tombs. The fangs of a death dog are prized components in necromantic rituals.

giant_eagle.name: giant eagle
giant_eagle.lore: Giant eagles are proud and intelligent raptors that nest on the highest dungeon peaks and mountain crags. They are sometimes willing to parley with respectful adventurers and occasionally serve as aerial mounts. A single tail feather is worth more than most peasants earn in a year.

roc_chick.name: roc chick
roc_chick.lore: Even a roc chick is a formidable beast, standing taller than a horse and possessing a voracious appetite. The parents defend nesting grounds with world-shaking ferocity. An unhatched roc egg is worth enough gold to buy a small kingdom.

roc_adult.name: roc adult
roc_adult.lore: A fully grown roc blots out the sun and creates windstorms with a single wingbeat. Ancient rocs have been known to carry off elephants as snacks for their young. Legends say that every roc feather contains a wish, if one knows the right ritual to release it.

stirge_swarm.name: stirge swarm
stirge_swarm.lore: Stirges are bloodsucking mosquito-like creatures that travel in dense swarms, attaching to exposed flesh with barbed proboscises. A large enough swarm can drain a horse dry in minutes. Their wings make a high-pitched whine that alerts dungeon veterans to take cover.

tengu_warrior.name: tengu warrior
tengu_warrior.lore: Tengu warriors are crow-headed humanoid creatures who are master swordsmen of legendary skill. They occupy high dungeon places near wind channels and are fiercely territorial. Some rare tengu serve as sword-instructors to mortals who prove themselves worthy.

thunderbird_lesser.name: lesser thunderbird
thunderbird_lesser.lore: The lesser thunderbird is a magnificent storm-hawk that generates electrical fields across its plumage. Each wingbeat crackles with arcs of lightning and its cries shake stone walls. Its feathers remain charged for months after the bird's death, making them prized magical components.

pixie_swarm.name: pixie swarm
pixie_swarm.lore: Individually harmless, a swarm of pixies is a nuisance capable of putting entire parties to sleep with enchanted dust. They delight in mischief and have an instinctive hostility toward heavily armed visitors. Pixie dust collected after they depart retains potent soporific properties.

redcap.name: redcap
redcap.lore: Redcaps are murderous fey that dye their caps in the blood of their victims and must kill regularly or fade away. They are supernaturally strong for their diminutive size, favoring heavy iron boots and long pikes. Their iron-shod feet can crush stone, and their laughter is deeply unsettling.

will_o_wisp_lesser.name: will-o'-wisp (lesser)
will_o_wisp_lesser.lore: Lesser will-o'-wisps are ghostly lights that lure travelers into bogs and pits by mimicking torch light. They feed on the fear and despair of those they lead to doom. They are notoriously difficult to harm, as most weapons simply pass through their luminous forms.

green_hag.name: green hag
green_hag.lore: Green hags dwell in fetid swamps and use powerful illusions to appear beautiful until they are close enough to strike. They subsist on misery, feeding emotionally on despair they deliberately cause. Covens of three hags can perform terrifying magical rituals far beyond their individual capabilities.

sea_hag.name: sea hag
sea_hag.lore: Sea hags are hideous aquatic fey whose faces are so terrible that a prolonged gaze can strike a creature dead of fright. They lurk in coastal caves and underwater dungeons, collecting trinkets from drowned sailors. Their appearance is so awful that mirrors break in their presence.

annis_hag.name: annis hag
annis_hag.lore: Annis hags are massive, iron-skinned fey who prefer brute force over trickery. A single annis can overpower a giant in a wrestling match, and their claws can shred metal armor. They wear cloaks made from the skins of their victims and smell of blood and iron.

night_hag.name: night hag
night_hag.lore: Night hags are among the most feared beings in existence, traveling the ethereal plane to torment sleeping mortals. They collect the souls of those they drive to evil in soul-trapping gems called heartstones. Any witch seeking to ascend must first broker a deal with a night hag.

satyr_trickster.name: satyr trickster
satyr_trickster.lore: Satyr tricksters are hedonistic goat-men who use enchanted music to disorient and rob travelers. They rarely seek to kill, preferring to steal valuables and vanish into the forest laughing. Their panpipes are occasionally left behind as powerful magical instruments.

dryad_guardian.name: dryad guardian
dryad_guardian.lore: A dryad guardian is an arboreal fey who has chosen to actively defend her grove rather than hide within her tree. She can step between trees at will and commands the vegetation of her domain as extensions of her own body. Those who threaten her forest find roots rising to bind their feet.

leprechaun_hostile.name: hostile leprechaun
leprechaun_hostile.lore: Not all leprechauns are willing to grant wishes — some who have been robbed too many times have become openly hostile to adventurers. They use powerful illusions and misdirection before lashing out with surprising ferocity. Despite their size their pots of gold are very real.

spriggan.name: spriggan
spriggan.lore: Spriggans are grotesque fairy guards who can expand to enormous size to protect their masters' treasures. In their enlarged form they possess tremendous strength but lose much of their natural agility. They are notorious thieves who hoard the stolen goods of travelers.

boggart.name: boggart
boggart.lore: Boggarts are mischievous household fey that have turned malevolent, taking delight in spoiling food, pinching sleepers, and causing minor disasters. They rarely fight directly, preferring to haunt and confuse before fleeing into shadows. A boggart attached to a dungeon room can make that room nearly uninhabitable.

fire_elemental.name: fire elemental
fire_elemental.lore: Summoned from the Plane of Fire or born from eruptions deep in the earth, fire elementals are columns of living flame possessed of malevolent intelligence. They leave scorched floors and melted walls in their wake. The blazing core that persists after their dispersal can power magical devices for decades.

water_elemental.name: water elemental
water_elemental.lore: Water elementals rise from deep underground lakes and flooded dungeon chambers, taking the form of towering churning waves. They drag victims beneath the surface and hold them until they stop struggling. The core of a water elemental remains perpetually cold and is sought after by cryomancers.

earth_elemental.name: earth elemental
earth_elemental.lore: Earth elementals are slow-moving colossi of living rock and soil that rise from dungeon walls. Their blows can shatter columns and collapse passages. The dense mineral core at their heart is a remarkable magical battery capable of storing enormous amounts of arcane energy.

air_elemental.name: air elemental
air_elemental.lore: Air elementals are near-invisible whirlwinds that can move at incredible speed through dungeon corridors. Their invisible slashing winds can strip flesh from bone in moments. The compressed air core at their center is prized by enchanters for use in flight-related magic items.

ice_elemental.name: ice elemental
ice_elemental.lore: Ice elementals are crystalline giants born from the deepest glacial caverns and the most savage arctic storms. Their touch coats victims in a sheath of magical ice that slows their movements. The ice core at their heart never melts and remains supernaturally cold even in volcanic environments.

magma_elemental.name: magma elemental
magma_elemental.lore: Magma elementals rise from volcanic vents in the deep dungeon, their ponderous bodies of living rock suffused with molten fire. Everything they touch ignites, and their footprints remain glowing for hours. A magma elemental core is among the most coveted items in pyromantic alchemy.

storm_elemental.name: storm elemental
storm_elemental.lore: Storm elementals are rare confluences of air and lightning given terrible form and will. They ride currents of electrified air through dungeon chimneys, striking with bolts of lightning. Their crackling core stores enough electrical energy to power a city for a year.

void_elemental.name: void elemental
void_elemental.lore: Void elementals originate from the space between planes, manifesting as patches of absolute darkness that devour light and sound. Their touch unravels the bonds of matter, leaving victims partially phased out of existence. A void shard from their core is one of the most dangerous substances in existence.

shambling_mound.name: shambling mound
shambling_mound.lore: Shambling mounds are massive ambulatory heaps of rotting vegetation animated by some swampy life-force or lightning strike. They crush and engulf prey, slowly digesting organic matter within their bulk. Lightning actually heals and invigorates them rather than causing damage.

assassin_vine.name: assassin vine
assassin_vine.lore: Assassin vines lurk motionless in dungeon gardens and along corridors, indistinguishable from ordinary vegetation. When prey passes within reach, tendrils shoot out to grab and constrict with crushing force. Their sap contains a mild paralytic useful in herbalist preparations.

thorn_slinger.name: thorn slinger
thorn_slinger.lore: Thorn slingers are sedentary plants that expel barrages of barbed, poison-coated thorns when any creature enters their range. They position themselves at chokepoints and room entrances for maximum effect. The thorns retain their poison for weeks after being expelled from the plant.

myconid_worker.name: myconid worker
myconid_worker.lore: Myconid workers are ambulatory mushroom folk who tend the vast underground gardens of their colonies. They are peaceful by nature but will defend their circles by releasing clouds of hallucinogenic spores. Their spores are a primary component in potions of confusion and certain dream remedies.

myconid_sovereign.name: myconid sovereign
myconid_sovereign.lore: Myconid sovereigns are ancient, enormous mushroom kings who govern entire subterranean fungal kingdoms through psychic spore networks. Their mind-altering spores can suppress the will of entire adventuring parties. The cap of a slain sovereign grows back as a fully sentient mushroom forest.

yellow_musk_creeper.name: yellow musk creeper
yellow_musk_creeper.lore: The yellow musk creeper produces intoxicating clouds of yellow pollen that overwhelm the minds of victims, drawing them into range of its grasping tendrils. Mindless thralls under its influence protect it and carry decomposing organic matter to fertilize its roots. The musk pollen alone is enough to drive most animals into docile confusion.

gas_spore.name: gas spore
gas_spore.lore: Gas spores are floating fungal pods that superficially resemble beholders, leading to fatal cases of mistaken identity among dungeon veterans. Any puncture causes them to detonate in a cloud of toxic spores. Those slain by the gas spore become hosts for new pods within hours.

violet_fungus.name: violet fungus
violet_fungus.lore: Violet fungi lurk in the dark, extending their tendrils to deliver a rotting touch that causes flesh to rapidly decay. They are often found clustered near gray ooze, with which they share a symbiotic relationship. In small quantities their extract is used in controlled decomposition experiments by alchemists.

pegasus_corrupted.name: corrupted pegasus
pegasus_corrupted.lore: Once a noble steed of the gods, this pegasus was twisted by shadow magic into a creature of darkness. Its formerly white flanks are now mottled black and its eyes glow a sickly purple. A courageous wizard who breaks the curse might restore it — but few survive the attempt.

unicorn_corrupted.name: corrupted unicorn
unicorn_corrupted.lore: A unicorn whose pure heart was corrupted by prolonged exposure to shadow energies, its once-golden horn now a jagged black spiral that drips with shadow-venom. It rages through dungeons destroying anything that once reminded it of its former life. The corrupted horn fragment retains terrible magical power.

sphinx_lesser.name: lesser sphinx
sphinx_lesser.lore: Lesser sphinxes guard ancient vaults and archives, compelling all who approach to answer riddles on pain of death. Unlike the great sphinxes, they lack godlike patience and grow violent when bored. Their accumulated knowledge of arcane lore makes defeating one feel like losing an irreplaceable library.

nue.name: nue
nue.lore: The nue is a terrifying chimeric creature from eastern legend with the head of a monkey, the body of a tanuki, the legs of a tiger, and the tail of a serpent. It brings misfortune and illness, and its shriek is said to induce nightmares in all who hear it. A quill from its serpentine tail is a powerful talisman against illness.

peryton.name: peryton
peryton.lore: The peryton is a winged stag of terrible omen whose shadow takes the shape of a human rather than its own form. It hunts intelligent prey specifically, drawn by some dark need to harvest humanoid hearts. The antlers of a peryton are said to grant prophetic visions when carved into a drinking cup.

carbuncle.name: carbuncle
carbuncle.lore: The carbuncle is a small, fox-like magical creature with a brilliant gemstone embedded in its forehead. It uses the gem to flash blinding light at predators before fleeing at tremendous speed. The gem, if carefully extracted after death, is among the most powerful magical focal points that can be found in nature.

sea_dragon_spawn.name: sea dragon spawn
sea_dragon_spawn.lore: Sea dragon spawn are juvenile dragons of the deep ocean, capable of pressurizing water into devastating focused jets. Even as young dragons they dwarf most surface creatures and possess considerable cunning. Their scales are among the most prized armor materials in any coastal nation.
